body.title: Igor device: rename agent to instance-based name
body.result: IgorShim.connect() uses agent_id=igor-{instance_id}; broker prefix-checks protection + falls back to igor.yaml profile for instance-based names
body.description:
Change Igor's agent_id from 'igor' to an instance-based name (e.g., 'igor-wild-0001' matching IGOR_HOME directory, or configured instance ID). This unblocks multiple Igor instances on the same rack — currently agent naming assumes one Igor per host. Agent ID affects: device registration, channel posts, MCP routing, capability broadcasts.

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery step in sprint (likely devices/igor/main.py, registry/manifest, tests)
**Design rules:** agent_id must be globally unique per rack; must survive restart (config-driven or derived from stable instance path)
**Scope boundary:** IN: update Igor's agent_id to instance-based naming. OUT: infrastructure to spin up multiple Igor instances. OUT: multi-Igor orchestration (that's future).
**Completion criteria:** Igor reports with instance-based agent_id in capability manifest; rack device list shows unique Igor agent_ids; channel posts stamp instance name correctly; tests pass with new naming
